system:
You are the Stage-1 Intake & Classification agent for Keysight SalesOps. Read one customer email and emit ONE strict JSON object. Output goes directly to json.loads() and enum validators.

OUTPUT CONTRACT — emit exactly these 10 keys:
{
  "language": "<"en", "es", "ja", "other">",
  "language_reasoning": "<string>",
  "intent": "<"po_intake", "quote_to_order", "trade_change_order", "ssd_change_request", "hold_release", "delivery_change", "service_order", "wo_update_request", "wo_status_inquiry", "service_contract_request", "general_inquiry", "out_of_scope", "spam">",
  "intent_confidence": <number 0.0-1.0>,
  "intent_reasoning": "<1-2 sentences citing exact words from the email>",
  "secondary_intents": [],
  "spam": <true|false>,
  "spam_reason": "<string; "" if not spam>",
  "summary": "<one English sentence>",
  "track_hint": "<trade|som|service_contract|none>"
}

FIELD RULES (positive form):
  • "intent" is a single string from the canonical list (never an array, never inside another object).
  • "intent_confidence" is a top-level number (never inside an "intents" object).
  • "track_hint" is the workflow track — one of: trade, som, service_contract, none. NOT an autonomy tier (no "L4_auto", "L3", "auto").
  • "intent_reasoning" is a sentence quoting words from the email (the field name is "intent_reasoning", not "notes" or "rationale").
  • Emit all 10 keys every time. Use "" or [] for empty values.

INTENT MAPPING (track in parens):
  "po_intake" (track=trade) — new purchase order received from a customer (no prior quote referenced)
  "quote_to_order" (track=trade) — customer asking to convert an existing quote into a sales order
  "trade_change_order" (track=trade) — customer requesting changes to an EXISTING booked sales order (qty, line items, billing) — NOT just a ship-date change
  "ssd_change_request" (track=trade) — customer requesting a Ship Schedule Date (SSD) change on an existing order — push out, pull in, or partial
  "hold_release" (track=trade) — customer asking to release an order from a hold (credit, export, payment)
  "delivery_change" (track=trade) — customer requesting a one-time delivery rescheduling (use ssd_change_request when they say 'ship schedule date' explicitly)
  "service_order" (track=som) — new request to create a work order — calibration, repair, installation, on-site service, possibly multi-asset
  "wo_update_request" (track=som) — customer asking to update or modify an EXISTING work order (add notes, add tasks, update assets)
  "wo_status_inquiry" (track=som) — customer asking about the status of an existing work order or open WOs
  "service_contract_request" (track=service_contract) — customer asking for a service contract / support agreement quote or order (cal plan, onsite plan, PM plan)
  "general_inquiry" (track=none) — other legitimate business question (EOL roadmap, product info, lead-time questions, etc.)
  "out_of_scope" (track=none) — legitimate but non-customer-business — automated notifications (Google/Microsoft security, AWS, GitHub), social-network alerts (LinkedIn), n…
  "spam" (track=none) — unsolicited / phishing / off-topic / promotional
  Trade = orders/PO/quote conversion/holds/ship-date changes. SOM = work-orders/calibration/repair/install. service_contract = cal plan/PM plan/support agreement. none = general inquiry, out_of_scope, spam.

OUT_OF_SCOPE vs SPAM — pick one carefully:
  • out_of_scope = LEGITIMATE non-customer-business from a KNOWN/established sender. The sender is a real brand or person (Google, Microsoft, AWS, GitHub, LinkedIn, the user's own company HR/IT, a Keysight teammate, a known vendor). Content is informational, transactional, or promotional but the sender is RECOGNIZABLE and not lookalike. Examples include: account-security alerts, billing notifications, calendar invites, newsletter forwards, "free trial" offers from real brands, internal HR reminders, out-of-office auto-replies, vendor receipts.
  • spam = UNSOLICITED material from UNKNOWN or LOOKALIKE senders, OR clearly malicious content. Phishing credential traps ("verify your account or it will be suspended"), wire-fraud setups ("our banking details have changed"), 419/advance-fee scams, lookalike-domain attacks (faux-keysight.com), unsolicited promo blasts from senders the recipient never opted into.
  • Decision rule: 
      - Forwarded newsletters / marketing from a real brand the recipient knows (Google, MS, AWS, LinkedIn, etc.) → out_of_scope, NOT spam.
      - "70% OFF" promo blast from no-name unknown-domain sender → spam.
      - Phishing / wire-fraud regardless of sender appearance → spam.
  • Both route to discard, but distinguishing them lets compliance/security flag actual phishing separately from harmless newsletter noise.

WORKED EXAMPLES (reproduce this exact shape):

Example 1 — fresh PO (po_intake / trade)
  IN  Subject "PO PO-X-1 — DSO purchase, please ack" / Body "PO-X-1 attached for one DSO. Net 45."
  OUT {"language":"en","language_reasoning":"All-English.","intent":"po_intake","intent_confidence":0.95,"intent_reasoning":"Subject and body explicitly attach PO PO-X-1; no quote referenced.","secondary_intents":[],"spam":false,"spam_reason":"","summary":"New PO PO-X-1 needing acknowledgment.","track_hint":"trade"}

Example 2 — quote-to-order (quote_to_order / trade)
  IN  Subject "Convert quote QT-A-1 → order — PO attached" / Body "Please convert QT-A-1 into an order using PO-A-1. Send SOA."
  OUT {"language":"en","language_reasoning":"All-English.","intent":"quote_to_order","intent_confidence":0.97,"intent_reasoning":"Body says 'convert QT-A-1 into an order' with PO attached.","secondary_intents":[],"spam":false,"spam_reason":"","summary":"Convert quote QT-A-1 to a sales order.","track_hint":"trade"}

Example 3 — service order (service_order / som)
  IN  Subject "Multi-asset cal request" / Body "Please open a work order to calibrate 6 instruments on-site, ISO 17025 traceable."
  OUT {"language":"en","language_reasoning":"All-English.","intent":"service_order","intent_confidence":0.96,"intent_reasoning":"Body says 'Please open a work order to calibrate' — new on-site cal request.","secondary_intents":[],"spam":false,"spam_reason":"","summary":"On-site calibration work order for 6 instruments.","track_hint":"som"}

Example 4 — promotional spam (spam / none)
  IN  Subject "🎉 70% OFF — TODAY ONLY" / Body "Click now to save big!"
  OUT {"language":"en","language_reasoning":"All-English.","intent":"spam","intent_confidence":0.99,"intent_reasoning":"Promotional emoji and urgency 'TODAY ONLY'; no order content.","secondary_intents":[],"spam":true,"spam_reason":"Promotional discount blast.","summary":"Promotional discount blast.","track_hint":"none"}

Example 5 — Google security alert (out_of_scope / none) — LEGITIMATE NOTIFICATION, NOT SPAM
  IN  From "Google <no-reply@accounts.google.com>" Subject "Security alert" / Body "App password created to sign in to your account. If you didn't generate this password, someone might be using your account."
  OUT {"language":"en","language_reasoning":"All-English.","intent":"out_of_scope","intent_confidence":0.97,"intent_reasoning":"Sender is google.com (legitimate); content is an account-security notification about app-password creation; no customer order or service request.","secondary_intents":[],"spam":false,"spam_reason":"","summary":"Google account security notification — app password created.","track_hint":"none"}

Example 6 — internal HR email (out_of_scope / none)
  IN  From "hr@keysight.com" Subject "Open enrollment reminder" / Body "Reminder: benefits open enrollment closes Friday. Log into Workday to make selections."
  OUT {"language":"en","language_reasoning":"All-English.","intent":"out_of_scope","intent_confidence":0.95,"intent_reasoning":"Internal HR reminder about benefits enrollment; not a customer-business request.","secondary_intents":[],"spam":false,"spam_reason":"","summary":"Internal HR reminder about benefits open enrollment.","track_hint":"none"}

Example 7 — forwarded marketing newsletter from a known brand (out_of_scope / none, NOT spam)
  IN  From "rituraj@leewayhertz.com" (forwarded) Subject "Fwd: Work faster with AI built into Google Workspace" / Body "Forwarded message from Google Workspace x YourStory: Try Google Workspace free for 14 days. Start your free trial."
  OUT {"language":"en","language_reasoning":"All-English.","intent":"out_of_scope","intent_confidence":0.96,"intent_reasoning":"Forwarded promotional newsletter from a known brand (Google Workspace) about a free-trial offer; sender is recognizable, not phishing or lookalike.","secondary_intents":[],"spam":false,"spam_reason":"","summary":"Forwarded Google Workspace marketing newsletter with free-trial offer.","track_hint":"none"}

Now read the email below and emit one JSON object matching the contract exactly. No prose, no code fences, no additional keys.

INTENT-CONFIDENCE RUBRIC (apply this to score the chosen intent):
  Start with base = 0.50 (uninformed prior).
  Then EVALUATE EACH RULE BELOW against this email and decide matched=true|false.
  Final intent_confidence = base + sum(delta where matched=true), clamped to [0.0, 1.0].
  EMIT one entry in `intent_confidence_breakdown` for EVERY rule below — even rules that don't match (matched=false, delta=0.0, evidence="").
  When matched=true, set delta to the rule's weight for the chosen intent (use per-intent override if present, otherwise default_delta) and put a SHORT quote from the email in `evidence`.

  • attachment_consistent [trigger, default +0.05] (per-intent overrides: po_intake=+0.10, quote_to_order=+0.10, service_order=+0.07, wo_status_inquiry=+0.00, general_inquiry=+0.00, out_of_scope=+0.00) — Attachments (PO PDF, BOM XLSX, ATP DOCX, calibration cert, etc.) match the document types the chosen intent typically carries. e.g., for po_intake: PO PDF, BOM XLSX, ATP DOCX
  • body_action_verb_match [trigger, default +0.20] — Email body uses the action verb the chosen intent expects — the customer states what they want done in clear language. e.g., for po_intake: acknowledge, issue SOA, confirm receipt
  • referenced_id_present [trigger, default +0.15] (per-intent overrides: po_intake=+0.20, quote_to_order=+0.20, trade_change_order=+0.18, ssd_change_request=+0.18, hold_release=+0.18, wo_update_request=+0.18) — A PO / Quote / Sales-Order / Work-Order / Asset / Invoice / Service-Contract identifier is present in the email body or attachments and matches the expected pattern for the chosen intent. e.g., for po_intake: PO-XYZ-123-…
  • subject_explicit_signal [trigger, default +0.30] (per-intent overrides: po_intake=+0.32, quote_to_order=+0.32, service_order=+0.32, spam=+0.40, out_of_scope=+0.35) — Subject contains a canonical token unique to the chosen intent — an unambiguous keyword that nearly always means this intent. e.g., for po_intake: PO PO-…, Purchase Order, OC PO-…
  • no_multi_intent_ambiguity [clearance, default +0.10] — The email is single-purpose — no other canonical intent's signals score strongly. This rule rewards clarity.
  • contradictory_attachment [penalty, default -0.10] (per-intent overrides: po_intake=-0.15, quote_to_order=-0.15) — The attachment type contradicts the chosen intent — e.g., quote_to_order intent but no quote reference present, or PO without line items, or service_order without an asset.
  • multi_intent_ambiguity [penalty, default -0.20] — Two or more canonical intents both look plausible (signals for both score above 0.60). Drop confidence to surface the ambiguity for human review.
  • vague_or_generic_body [penalty, default -0.15] (per-intent overrides: general_inquiry=-0.05, out_of_scope=-0.05) — Body is too short, too generic, or lacks specifics to confidently infer intent. Often happens with sender-only signals.
user:
EMAIL THREAD (chronological — ROOT drives intent, replies are clarifying context):
--- MESSAGE 1 [ROOT (primary intent source)] ---
From: aaron.brewer@bluehawkdefense.com
Date: 2026-05-06T15:14:20.585205
Subject: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold

Hi team,

Our order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 went on compliance hold last week. What's needed to clear it? We need delivery before May 30.

Aaron Brewer
Program Manager

--- MESSAGE 2 [REPLY 1] ---
From: ZBrain Sales Ops Desk <orderinbox@leewayhertz.com>
Date: 2026-05-06T15:40:20.585205
Subject: Re: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold

Hi Aaron,

Hold reason on file: ECCN classification mismatch on line 1 (KS-SA-260227). Our trade compliance flagged that the destination indicated on the PO doesn't match the BIS license on file for that ECCN.

To release we'll need either:
  • Confirmation that ship-to is the original DD address (Falls Church, VA), or
  • A new BIS authorization referencing the alternate destination.

ZBrain Sales Ops

--- MESSAGE 3 [REPLY 2] ---
From: aaron.brewer@bluehawkdefense.com
Date: 2026-05-06T16:53:20.585205
Subject: Re: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold

Confirmed — ship-to is the original Falls Church VA DD address. There's no destination change. I'll have our trade compliance officer email you a signed end-use statement today.

--- MESSAGE 4 [REPLY 3] ---
From: ZBrain Sales Ops Desk <orderinbox@leewayhertz.com>
Date: 2026-05-06T17:31:20.585205
Subject: Re: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold

Got it. Once we have the signed end-use statement we can release the hold.
FYI we'll route this for legal review (24h SLA). I'll confirm release to you the moment that lands.

--- MESSAGE 5 [REPLY 4] ---
From: aaron.brewer@bluehawkdefense.com
Date: 2026-05-06T21:31:20.585205
Subject: Re: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold

Trade compliance just sent the end-use statement to your team. Please let me know if anything else is needed.

--- MESSAGE 6 [REPLY 5] ---
From: ZBrain Sales Ops Desk <orderinbox@leewayhertz.com>
Date: 2026-05-06T21:43:20.585205
Subject: Re: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold

Received the end-use statement. Forwarded to legal. Will confirm by end of business tomorrow.

--- MESSAGE 7 [REPLY 6] ---
From: ZBrain Sales Ops Desk <orderinbox@leewayhertz.com>
Date: 2026-05-07T21:13:20.585205
Subject: Re: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold

Update — legal cleared the hold. Releasing SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 now.
Updated promised ship date: May 26. Carrier: FedEx Priority Overnight.
Tracking will follow once the warehouse picks it.

--- MESSAGE 8 [REPLY 7] ---
From: aaron.brewer@bluehawkdefense.com
Date: 2026-05-07T21:21:20.585205
Subject: Re: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold

Thanks — appreciate the fast turn. Standing by for tracking.

PRIMARY ENVELOPE OF THE LATEST MESSAGE THAT TRIGGERED THIS PIPELINE:
FROM: aaron.brewer@bluehawkdefense.com
SUBJECT: Re: Order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 — release request after compliance hold
LANGUAGE_HINT: en

Classify the THREAD's intent (read from the ROOT message). The latest message may be a CSR confirmation or buyer follow-up that does not represent the original ask. Use the full chain to disambiguate. Output one JSON object only.

language.description: ISO 639-1 code from the canonical list

language_reasoning.description: 1 sentence explaining the language cue (script, vocabulary, idiom)

intent.description: Single canonical intent value — never an array, never invented

intent_confidence.description: Final confidence after applying the rubric, clamped to [0, 1]. MUST equal base + sum of matched deltas.

intent_confidence_base.description: The starting prior from the KB rubric (typically 0.50). Echo it back as-is.

intent_confidence_breakdown.description: Per-rule breakdown of how the confidence was scored. EMIT ONE ENTRY FOR EVERY RUBRIC RULE — even rules that didn't match. Order: triggers first, then clearance, then penalties.

rule_key.description: Identifier of the rubric rule being scored

matched.description: True if the email satisfies this rule

delta.description: Contribution to confidence — the rule's effective delta (0.0 when matched=false)

evidence.description: Short quote from the email or attachment that justifies matched=true; empty string when matched=false

intent_reasoning.description: 1–2 sentences citing exact words from the email subject or body

secondary_intents.description: Other plausible intents beyond the primary; empty array if none

spam.description: True iff the email is spam/phishing/promotional

spam_reason.description: Why it's spam; empty string if not spam

summary.description: One English sentence summarizing the primary request

track_hint.description: Workflow track: trade=orders/PO/holds; som=work-orders/cal/repair; service_contract=cal-plan/PM-plan; none=general/spam

raw_response: {"language":"en","language_reasoning":"The thread is written in English with business/order terminology (e.g., 'compliance hold', 'release the hold').","intent":"hold_release","intent_confidence":1.0,"intent_confidence_base":0.5,"intent_confidence_breakdown":[{"rule_key":"attachment_consistent","matched":false,"delta":0.0,"evidence":""},{"rule_key":"body_action_verb_match","matched":true,"delta":0.2,"evidence":"\"went on compliance hold\" / \"What's needed to clear it?\""},{"rule_key":"referenced_id_present","matched":true,"delta":0.18,"evidence":"\"SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002\""},{"rule_key":"subject_explicit_signal","matched":true,"delta":0.3,"evidence":"\"release request after compliance hold\""},{"rule_key":"no_multi_intent_ambiguity","matched":true,"delta":0.1,"evidence":"Single request focused on clearing/releasing the compliance hold for the order."},{"rule_key":"contradictory_attachment","matched":false,"delta":0.0,"evidence":""},{"rule_key":"multi_intent_ambiguity","matched":false,"delta":0.0,"evidence":""},{"rule_key":"vague_or_generic_body","matched":false,"delta":0.0,"evidence":""}],"intent_reasoning":"Root message says the order \"went on compliance hold\" and asks \"What's needed to clear it?\" with a \"release request\" for SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002.","secondary_intents":[],"spam":false,"spam_reason":"","summary":"Customer requests guidance and action to clear/release a compliance hold on sales order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 to meet a May 30 delivery need.","track_hint":"trade"}

parsed.language_reasoning: The thread is written in English with business/order terminology (e.g., 'compliance hold', 'release the hold').

intent_confidence_breakdown.evidence: "went on compliance hold" / "What's needed to clear it?"
Single request focused on clearing/releasing the compliance hold for the order.

parsed.intent_reasoning: Root message says the order "went on compliance hold" and asks "What's needed to clear it?" with a "release request" for SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002.
parsed.summary: Customer requests guidance and action to clear/release a compliance hold on sales order SO-BLUEH-DEF-021-2002 to meet a May 30 delivery need.
